input: Patient is a 67-year-old male presenting with progressive shortness of breath over 3 days, bilateral leg swelling, and orthopnea. History of hypertension and previous MI.
expected_output: Elderly male with HF exacerbation. Key findings: dyspnea, bilateral edema, orthopnea. Background: HTN, prior MI. Likely decompensated heart failure requiring diuresis.

input: 35-year-old female with sudden-onset severe headache described as 'worst of my life', neck stiffness, photophobia, and fever of 38.9°C. No prior headache history.
expected_output: Young female with thunderclap headache, meningismus, photophobia, and fever. Red flag presentation — rule out subarachnoid hemorrhage and bacterial meningitis urgently.

input: 52-year-old diabetic male reports 3-day history of burning on urination, increased frequency, and cloudy urine. Temperature 37.2°C, no flank pain or rigors.
expected_output: Diabetic male with lower urinary tract infection symptoms — dysuria, frequency, cloudy urine, afebrile. No upper UTI features. Likely uncomplicated UTI; urine culture recommended.

input: 19-year-old female with 6-week history of fatigue, pallor, palpitations, and heavy menstrual periods. Conjunctival pallor on exam. No bleeding elsewhere.
expected_output: Young female with symptomatic anemia (fatigue, pallor, palpitations) in context of menorrhagia. Presentation consistent with iron-deficiency anemia; CBC and iron studies warranted.

input: Obese 48-year-old male complaining of loud snoring, witnessed apneas by partner, daytime somnolence, and morning headaches. Neck circumference 44 cm, BMI 34.
expected_output: Middle-aged obese male with classic obstructive sleep apnea triad: witnessed apneas, hypersomnolence, snoring. Risk factors: obesity, large neck. Referral for polysomnography recommended.

input: 72-year-old female with confusion starting this morning, decreased urine output for 2 days, dry mucous membranes, HR 102, BP 88/60. Resides in nursing home.
expected_output: Elderly female with acute confusion, oliguria, tachycardia, hypotension, and signs of dehydration. Presentation consistent with sepsis or severe dehydration; IV fluids and sepsis workup urgently needed.

input: 28-year-old male athlete presents with anterior knee pain worsening with stair climbing and prolonged sitting. No trauma. Pain reproduced by palpation of the patellar tendon.
expected_output: Young male athlete with anterior knee pain at patellar tendon insertion, exacerbated by activity and sitting. Clinical picture consistent with patellar tendinopathy; physio and load management recommended.

input: 61-year-old smoker with 6-week history of hoarseness, mild dysphagia, and 4 kg unintentional weight loss. No prior ENT history. Smoker for 30 years.
expected_output: Elderly smoker with persistent hoarseness, dysphagia, and significant weight loss — red-flag symptoms. High suspicion for laryngeal or hypopharyngeal malignancy; urgent ENT referral and laryngoscopy.
